risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "Two randomisation lists are computer generated by a statistician otherwise not involved in the study." Comment: This explicitly describes a proper random sequence generation method (computer-generated randomisation) for cluster allocation of practices, which meets criteria for low risk.

Quote: "Two randomisation lists are computer generated by a statistician otherwise not involved in the study. The lists are stored by the two regional study coordinators. After the definitive inclusion of a general practice into the study, the coordinator of the respective region calls the coordinator of the other region who, after registering the practice, hands out the allocation information." Comment: This describes a central/remote allocation procedure in which allocation information is released only after the practice has been definitively included, which would prevent the enrolling coordinator from knowing the upcoming assignment beforehand.

risk_of_bias.domain: Blinding of participants and personnel (performance bias)
risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "If the practice is randomised to intervention program the study centre invites the trainer to a special course lasting 8 hours." and "Fall prevention program (intervention group only)" and "The intervention investigated in PreFalls is a 16 week program with focus on fall prevention." Comment: This is an exercise/CBT-like behavioural intervention delivered only to the intervention group, so participants and personnel delivering the intervention would necessarily know allocation. Several key study outcomes are plausibly influenced by this knowledge, including self-reported fear of falling (FES-I), quality of life (EuroQol), physical activity (PAR), and even fall reporting via daily diaries. Therefore lack of blinding with outcomes susceptible to performance effects indicates high risk.

Quote: "Falls are monitored by patients over the full study period with a daily fall calendar." and "For the full study period of 24 months participants are asked to fill in a fall diary." and "Fear of Falling is assessed with the German version of the Falls Efficacy Scale International (FES-I) ... physical activity by the 7-Day Physical Active Recall (PAR) ... quality of life with the EuroQol. Furthermore, patients are asked to rate their overall health status..." Comment: The outcome assessors include patient self-report for falls/fall diary and for subjective outcomes (FES-I, physical activity recall, EuroQol, health status). The intervention is an exercise/CBT-type program delivered only to the intervention group, so participants were necessarily aware of allocation; no blinding of participants or separate blinded assessors is described. Because at least one outcome is subjective and self-reported by unblinded participants, knowledge of allocation could influence outcome measurement. By the worst-outcome rule, this makes the domain High risk even though some physical performance tests are also reported.

Summary: This article is a study protocol/design paper and does not report observed post-randomization attrition numbers for any analysed outcome. It describes planned follow-up and planned handling of missing data, but not actual per-arm randomized versus analysed numbers, reasons for withdrawal, or outcome-specific loss at 4, 12, or 24 months. Relevant text includes: "For the full study period of 24 months participants are asked to fill in a fall diary... In case that during follow-up some patients stop to fill in the diary they will be phoned to ask for basic information on falls..." and "Analyses of the primary endpoint... will be performed on base of the full-analysis set (complete information regarding the primary outcome parameter)... A detailed statistical analysis plan (SAP) will be defined in advance to the final analysis, to fix detailed statistical considerations with regard to further data analyses, multiple test issue, missing value imputation and sensitivity analyses." Comment: Because only planned procedures are reported and no actual post-randomization missing outcome data by arm are available, attrition bias for the outcomes of interest cannot be judged as Low or High from this paper; per Cochrane guidance this is Unclear risk.
